act_name: The Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act
section_title: Decreasing adjustment by the supplier after the tax withheld at
content:
50. Decreasing adjustment by the supplier after the tax withheld at
source.—(1) If a tax is withheld at source‚ the registered person may‚ in the prescribed
manner‚ make a decreasing adjustment of an amount equal to the amount of money
withheld at source.
(2) The adjustment shall be claimed in the tax period in which the tax payable
on a supply is paid or within the next six months after such tax period is
over and a claim for such adjustment after such period shall be time-
barred.
Page 33 10/21/2013
(3) No decreasing adjustment shall be claimed by a supplier if he does not
issue a combined tax invoice, and withholding certificate in favour of the
withholding entity.
TAX INVOICES AND OTHER DOCUMENT

No decreasing adjustment shall be claimed by a supplier if he does not
issue a combined tax invoice, and withholding certificate in favour of the
